blurb:
Hamp and Harry's on Roswell Street in Marietta is one of those spots that actually lives up to the hype. The food, the atmosphere, and the service all hit the right notes -- and people who go for the first time tend to come back. It's a great choice for a proper date night or a dinner out when you want something that feels a little special without being stuffy.

The food gets real praise from regulars, and the space has a lot of warmth to it. Reviews mention the presentation and the overall experience as highlights. Even when it's busy, the staff seems to take care of people well.

Just know they're closed on Mondays. On Fridays and Saturdays they're open until 2am, which makes them one of the few solid late-night options in the area.
address: 168 Roswell St NE, Marietta, GA 30060, USA

blurb:
If you haven't made it to Buttermilk Kitchen yet, it needs to go on your weekend list. It's a breakfast and lunch spot on Roswell Road that does Southern comfort food from scratch, and everything is locally sourced. The vibe is casual and a little cozy, and it fills up fast on weekends, so go early or expect a wait.

The menu is the kind of Southern breakfast food that actually tastes homemade, not just themed that way. People rave about pretty much everything they order here, and the 4.6 rating across thousands of reviews backs that up.

Just know they're only open until 2pm on weekdays and 3pm on weekends, and they're closed Mondays. Plan accordingly or you'll show up to a locked door.
address: 4225 Roswell Rd NE, Atlanta, GA 30342, USA
hours: Monday: Closed, Tuesday: 8:00 AM – 2:00 PM, Wednesday: 8:00 AM – 2:00 PM, Thursday: 8:00 AM – 2:00 PM, Friday: 8:00 AM – 2:00 PM, Saturday: 8:00 AM – 3:00 PM, Sunday: 8:00 AM – 3:00 PM

blurb:
The Select in Sandy Springs is a dinner-focused spot with an Art Deco feel and an open kitchen where you can watch the cooks work. The menu leans toward modern European and the space feels polished without being uptight. It's the kind of place you'd bring out-of-town guests or go for a proper date night.

The conservatory area is a nice touch if you want a little more atmosphere. Given the style and setting, this is a good pick when you want something that feels a step above your usual weeknight spot.

They don't open until 4pm most days, and on Sunday they do brunch starting at 10am. On Thursdays through Saturdays they stay open late.
address: 6405 Blue Stone Rd Suite 200, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA
hours: Monday: 4:00 – 10:00 PM, Tuesday: 4:00 – 10:00 PM, Wednesday: 4:00 – 10:00 PM, Thursday: 4:00 – 11:00 PM, Friday: 4:00 PM – 12:00 AM, Saturday: 4:00 PM – 12:00 AM, Sunday: 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM

blurb:
The Chastain is the kind of place you take someone when you want to impress them without being stuffy about it. It's on Powers Ferry and has a great outdoor tent area that works year-round. The atmosphere is lively and the staff actually know the menu, which makes a difference.

People go back again and again for the burger, and the miso aioli that comes with the fries is worth mentioning on its own. The pastries at brunch are also a highlight, so don't skip them if you go on a weekend morning.

They serve breakfast and lunch every day, and dinner Tuesday through Sunday. It's on the pricier side, but this is a solid pick for a date night or a special occasion.
address: 4320 Powers Ferry Rd NW, Atlanta, GA 30342, USA

blurb:
Pacific Buffet on Chastain Meadows Pkwy is the kind of place that just works when you need to feed a group without any drama. It's all-you-can-eat with sushi, tempura, and American options, so even picky eaters usually find something they like. Good for family dinners when everyone wants something different.

Don't go in expecting anything fancy. The sushi and tempura are the highlights worth filling your plate with. At this price point, it's hard to complain.

They're open seven days a week and stay open a little later on Fridays and Saturdays, so it works as a weeknight dinner option without rushing.
address: 2475 Chastain Meadows Pkwy NW, Marietta, GA 30066, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 9:30 PM, Saturday: 11:00 AM – 9:30 PM, Sunday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM

blurb:
If you haven't been to Brazilian Bakery Cafe on Powers Ferry, you're missing one of the better casual spots in the area. It's bright, friendly, and the kind of place you pop into for a quick breakfast or afternoon coffee and end up staying longer than you planned. Good for a solo work-from-somewhere morning or a low-key weekend treat with the family.

The Brazilian light bites are worth trying even if you don't know what to order -- just point at something and you'll probably be happy. The traditional cakes and cookies are the real draw. People come back specifically for those.

They're open until 10pm every day, which is later than you'd expect from a bakery. Sundays they open at 8am instead of 6:30, so plan accordingly if you're an early riser.
address: 1260 Powers Ferry Rd A, Marietta, GA 30067, USA
hours: Monday: 6:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Tuesday: 6:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Wednesday: 6:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Thursday: 6:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Friday: 6:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Saturday: 6:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Sunday: 8:00 AM – 10:00 PM

blurb:
China Great Wall on Sandy Plains is your basic neighborhood Chinese spot -- nothing flashy, but it does the job when you want familiar takeout without much thought. Strip mall location, straightforward menu, and prices that won't hurt. Good for a quick weeknight dinner or a lazy Sunday when nobody wants to cook.

You're getting the classics here -- the kind of Chinese food most people grew up ordering. It's reliable and the portions are solid for the price.

Sundays they don't open until 3pm, so if you're planning an early weekend lunch, this isn't the one.
address: 1860 Sandy Plains Rd #7836, Marietta, GA 30066, USA

blurb:
Marietta Melt Yard on Whitlock is a solid pick when you want a real burger and a drink without driving far. The inside has a lot of personality -- they go all-out on seasonal decor -- and there's a back deck that's worth asking about when the weather cooperates. Good for a casual dinner out or grabbing a late bite on a Friday night.

The burgers are the main event here. They also accommodate gluten-free buns, which is a genuine plus if anyone in your group needs that. Drinks are good too, and the bar area works fine if you don't have a reservation.

They're open until 10pm on weekends, so it holds up as a later-evening option when most places nearby are already winding down.
address: 800 Whitlock Ave NW, Marietta, GA 30064, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Saturday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Sunday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM

blurb:
Johnny's Hideaway on Roswell Road is an Atlanta institution. It's a retro nightclub that's been around forever, covered in Sinatra and Elvis memorabilia, and it plays oldies all night long. If you've never been, it's worth a visit just to see the place.

The food is straightforward pub grub, nothing fancy, but that's not really why people come. You come for the vibe, the music, and the fact that it's genuinely unlike anything else around here.

They're open until 3am most nights, so this is your spot after a Braves game or a late night out when everywhere else has already closed.
address: 3771 Roswell Rd NE, Atlanta, GA 30342, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 3:00 AM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 3:00 AM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 3:00 AM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 3:00 AM, Friday: 12:00 PM – 3:00 AM, Saturday: 12:00 PM – 3:00 AM, Sunday: 12:00 PM – 12:00 AM

blurb:
Rio is a Brazilian all-you-can-eat steakhouse on Powers Ferry, and it's one of those places that's great for a group or a hungry family. The whole concept is relaxed and fun. Gauchos bring the meat around to your table and you just say yes or no. It's a good time.

The barbecue is the main event, but do not skip the cheese bread. It's warm and pillowy and people talk about it constantly. They also have feijoada, which is a hearty Brazilian black bean stew, if you want something beyond the meat.

They're open seven days a week and stay open late on weekends, so it works for a weeknight dinner or a Saturday night out with friends.
address: 1275 Powers Ferry Rd ste 230, Marietta, GA 30067, USA
hours: Monday: 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Thursday: 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Friday: 10:00 AM – 11:00 PM, Saturday: 10:00 AM – 11:00 PM, Sunday: 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM

restaurant_name: Banana Leaf ATL Thai • Sushi • Bar
blurb:
Banana Leaf in Sandy Springs does Thai and sushi, and people who go there tend to rave about the food. The Tom Yum and Tom Kha soups come up a lot in reviews -- both described as some of the best around. The kitchen clearly knows what it's doing.

The inside is clean and well-decorated, and the whole setup works for a nice dinner out or a low-key date night. It's a step above your average strip-mall Thai place in terms of atmosphere.

Just note they're closed on Mondays, and Sunday hours start at 3pm rather than lunchtime. Worth double-checking before you head over.
address: 227 Sandy Springs Pl NE Suite 502, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
The Wing Cafe on Roswell Road is the kind of place you go when you want wings, cold beer, and a good time. It's high energy and fun, with an outdoor tiki bar and live music that makes it a great spot for a night out with friends or a post-game hangout. The crowd is lively and the vibe matches.

The wings are the main event and they deliver. Pair them with a craft beer from the tap list and you're set. It's also a solid game day destination if you want to get out of the house.

They're open every day until 11pm, so it works for late dinners too.
address: 2145 Roswell Rd, Marietta, GA 30062, USA

restaurant_name: Kitchen + Kocktails By Kevin Kelley - Atlanta
blurb:
If you want upscale soul food and a lively atmosphere, this is the spot. It's inside Perimeter Mall, which sounds casual, but the vibe inside is anything but. People bring their families here for birthdays and special occasions, and it pulls it off well.

The food gets consistently high marks and the cocktail menu is a big part of the appeal. The service has a real reputation for being warm and attentive, which makes a difference when you're celebrating something.

It's open seven days a week with late hours on Fridays and Saturdays, so it works for a weeknight dinner or a night out when you don't want to rush.
address: 4400 Ashford Dunwoody Rd Space #3030, Dunwoody, Atlanta, GA 30346, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM, Saturday: 10:00 AM – 11:00 PM, Sunday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM

blurb:
Turmeric on Sandy Plains has built a strong following in the area, and the food is consistently good. It's a solid choice when you're in the mood for Indian and want something a step above the usual lunch buffet spots. The rating speaks for itself with nearly 700 reviews sitting at 4.6 stars.

The menu covers all the Indian classics and the kitchen takes its time with the flavors. It works well for a weeknight dinner out or a weekend meal with family. The price is reasonable for sit-down Indian food.

They are closed on Mondays, so plan accordingly.
address: 1043 Sandy Plains Rd E, Marietta, GA 30066, USA
hours: Monday: Closed, Tuesday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Wednesday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Thursday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Friday: 11:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Saturday: 12:00 – 10:00 PM, Sunday: 12:00 – 9:30 PM

blurb:
This one is a real hidden gem on Allgood Road. It's a Mexican grocery store with a taqueria in the back, and the food coming out of that kitchen is genuinely excellent. Don't let the setup fool you. The people who know about it keep coming back.

The al pastor is the move here. It's well seasoned, not too crispy, and cooked exactly right. The bean and cheese pupusas are 10 out of 10, and the chicken plate with rice and beans is worth ordering too. The beans are made from scratch, not from a can, and you can tell. The tortas are great as well if you want something handheld.

This is a cash-and-carry kind of spot, cheap and fast. Perfect for lunch or a quick weeknight dinner when you want real food without a big bill.
address: 786 Allgood Rd NE, Marietta, GA 30062, USA
hours: Monday: 9:00 AM – 9:50 PM, Tuesday: 9:00 AM – 9:50 PM, Wednesday: 9:00 AM – 9:50 PM, Thursday: 9:00 AM – 9:50 PM, Friday: 9:00 AM – 9:50 PM, Saturday: 9:00 AM – 9:50 PM, Sunday: 9:00 AM – 9:50 PM

blurb:
If you're craving Cajun-Creole food and don't want to drive far, Copeland's on Cobb Parkway is a solid choice. It's a New Orleans chain, so the menu covers a lot of ground -- gumbo, jambalaya, etouffee, the works. The signature cocktails are worth trying too.

It's a good pick for a casual dinner out or lunch with friends. The menu is long enough that everyone in the group will find something, which makes it easy when you're with a crowd.

They do brunch on Sundays starting at 9am, which is a nice option if you want something a little different on the weekend.
address: 3101 Cobb Pkwy SE Suite #220, Atlanta, GA 30339, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Saturday: 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Sunday: 9:00 AM – 10:00 PM

blurb:
This is a real neighborhood pub in Sandy Springs -- the kind of place you can go for a casual lunch or stick around for drinks after dinner. The food is solid pub fare and the drink menu covers what you'd expect from a good Irish pub.

The rooftop is the hidden gem here. It holds up to 60 people and you can reserve it for private events, which is great if you need a venue for a party or work gathering. People who've done it say it's a blast.

They close a little earlier on Sundays at 9pm, so keep that in mind if you're planning a weekend visit.
address: 227 Sandy Springs Pl NE Suite 416, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA
hours: Monday: 11:30 AM – 11:00 PM, Tuesday: 11:30 AM – 11:00 PM, Wednesday: 11:30 AM – 11:00 PM, Thursday: 11:30 AM – 11:00 PM, Friday: 11:30 AM – 11:30 PM, Saturday: 11:30 AM – 11:30 PM, Sunday: 11:30 AM – 9:00 PM

blurb:
Lemon Grass on Roswell Road is where we end up when we want Thai and don't feel like driving far. The dining room is spacious and comfortable, with warm red decor that makes it feel like more than your average strip mall spot. Good for a date night or a quiet weeknight dinner.

They do Thai noodles, curries, and all the classics, and they do them well. The portions are solid and the prices are easy on the wallet. It's the kind of place you can count on to be consistently good every time.

Just note they're closed on Sundays and they break between lunch and dinner service most days. So it's not the spot for a spontaneous late afternoon meal.
address: 2145 Roswell Rd, Marietta, GA 30062, USA

blurb:
Laredos is our go-to on Sandy Plains when we want solid Mexican food without a lot of fuss. The inside is colorful and festive, and it has that cantina feel that makes it work for a family dinner or a casual night out with friends. The margaritas are a big draw and worth ordering.

They do all the Mexican classics well. It's the kind of place where everyone at the table finds something they want, and the portions are generous for the price. Good for a weeknight when you don't want to cook but also don't want to spend a lot.

They're open every day until at least 10pm, which is handy when the dinner decision happens late.
address: 1860 Sandy Plains Rd, Marietta, GA 30066, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 10:30 PM, Saturday: 11:00 AM – 10:30 PM, Sunday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM

blurb:
Tre Vele in Sandy Springs is a good date night spot -- the inside is cozy and it has the kind of feel where you actually want to linger over dinner. The service tends to be attentive without being intrusive, which goes a long way.

The margarita pizza and chicken Parm are popular orders and both get solid reviews. If you're a fan of eggplant Parm, people order that one too. The Arancini is on the menu as a starter, though it's not for everyone -- the flavor is on the tangier side.

Fridays and Saturdays they're open until 11pm, so it works well if you want to make a night of it rather than rushing through dinner.
address: 6017 Sandy Springs Cir, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA
